question: What is the foundational objective of system security within an IT infrastructure?

options.a: To replace all physical server hardware with virtualized cloud instances.
options.b: To protect core components like operating systems, applications, and middleware through hardening.
options.c: To exclusively focus on perimeter firewalls to keep attackers outside the network.
options.d: To increase application performance by disabling security protocols that introduce latency.

question: Why did the WannaCry ransomware outbreak of 2017 pose such a massive threat to global systems?

options.a: It successfully bypassed all modern AI-based EDR tools by utilizing sophisticated behavioral mimicking.
options.b: It exploited a known vulnerability in the Windows SMB protocol for which a patch was already available.
options.c: It attacked the physical hardware BIOS of the affected servers to prevent system recovery.
options.d: It relied on zero-day exploits that had never been documented by security researchers previously.

question: Which of the following is a primary operating system attack vector described in the notes?

options.a: Physical destruction of server rack power supplies.
options.b: Unpatched vulnerabilities in system code and services.
options.c: The automatic replacement of local user profiles with root-level administrator accounts.
options.d: The deliberate throttling of network bandwidth to disrupt user sessions.

question: What makes middleware components particularly dangerous if left unsecured?

options.a: They act as a bridge between the OS and applications, often serving as a target for lateral movement.
options.b: They are incompatible with standard operating system logging and audit mechanisms.
options.c: They automatically delete system audit logs to conserve disk space for application data.
options.d: They can only run on legacy, non-encrypted hardware platforms.

question: Which of the following is an example of an attack vector specifically targeting middleware?

options.a: SQL injection aimed at manipulating database queries via application input.
options.b: The physical theft of server chassis from secure, locked-down colocation facilities.
options.c: The unauthorized replacement of server-side CPU processors with faster models.
options.d: Directly editing system kernel binaries to change the OS version string.

question: What is the primary goal of the 'System Hardening' process?

options.a: To reduce the system's attack surface by disabling unnecessary services, ports, and insecure defaults.
options.b: To replace all existing administrative accounts with a single global shared password.
options.c: To maximize hardware performance by overclocking the system's processing cores.
options.d: To ensure that all temporary files are permanently saved to the system registry.

question: What concept describes using multiple layers of security controls to build redundancy?

options.a: Defense in Depth.
options.b: The Principle of Least Privilege.
options.c: Network Segmentation.
options.d: Credential Obfuscation.

question: In the Linux security model, what is the purpose of Mandatory Access Control (MAC) frameworks like SELinux?

options.a: To define strict policies governing what a process can do, even if the user is root.
options.b: To automatically grant read/write access to all system logs for every local user.
options.c: To allow standard users to bypass system kernel-level permission checks during boot.
options.d: To encrypt the home directories of all users using public-key infrastructure.

question: How does Fail2ban provide automated protection for an internet-facing Linux server?

options.a: It monitors logs for repeated failed login attempts and bans the IP address via firewall rules.
options.b: It encrypts all network traffic arriving at the SSH port to prevent eavesdropping.
options.c: It automatically patches vulnerable software libraries found in the operating system.
options.d: It replaces insecure user passwords with physical smart card authentication tokens.

question: What is the function of Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) in Linux?

options.a: To control how users authenticate to the system and enforce policies like account locking.
options.b: To serve as a firewall that blocks all inbound traffic on ports below 1024.
options.c: To provide a graphical interface for managing user disk quotas and storage limits.
options.d: To automatically compress system logs to save disk space for security archives.

question: What access state does the Linux file permission '600' provide?

options.a: Read and write access for the owner only; no access for group or others.
options.b: Full read, write, and execute access for the owner, group, and others.
options.c: Read-only access for everyone on the system.
options.d: Execute-only access for the owner only.

question: In Linux, what command is used to assign a file to a specific user and group?

options.a: chown
options.b: chmod
options.c: chgrp
options.d: chperm

question: What is the role of the Security Reference Monitor (SRM) in Windows?

options.a: It enforces access control policies by checking every access request against the defined ACLs.
options.b: It automatically generates a graphical report of system performance metrics.
options.c: It manages the distribution of security updates from the WSUS server.
options.d: It serves as an antivirus scanner that monitors file downloads in real time.

question: What does User Account Control (UAC) do in a Windows environment?

options.a: Prompts users for elevated permissions when an action requires administrative rights.
options.b: Automatically blocks all internet access for standard user accounts.
options.c: Encrypts the system drive using a BitLocker recovery key.
options.d: Manages user account password rotations in Active Directory.

question: What is the primary function of BitLocker Drive Encryption?

options.a: It encrypts an entire storage volume to protect data at rest against unauthorized physical access.
options.b: It creates a virtual private network tunnel to secure data in transit.
options.c: It monitors for and blocks malicious processes running on the local host.
options.d: It provides centralized management for pushing security updates across a domain.

question: What tool is used in Windows environments to centrally manage security policies across thousands of machines?

options.a: Group Policy.
options.b: Task Manager.
options.c: Windows Defender Firewall.
options.d: System Information.

question: How does WSUS help organizations improve their security posture?

options.a: By enabling centralized control, distribution, and monitoring of security updates.
options.b: By encrypting all network traffic between internal clients and external servers.
options.c: By automatically detecting and removing malware from end-user email accounts.
options.d: By providing a graphical interface for configuring complex Active Directory forests.

question: What is the goal of setting strict file permissions on application configuration files?

options.a: To ensure only the necessary system users can read or modify sensitive credentials and keys.
options.b: To boost application performance by limiting the number of times files are read.
options.c: To prevent unauthorized users from deleting the file entirely.
options.d: To force the operating system to move the file into a hidden directory.

question: Which of the following describes an 'Indicators of Compromise' (IoC)?

options.a: Specific signs, such as unauthorized account creation, suggesting a breach may be in progress.
options.b: The standard password complexity requirements defined by the organization's HR team.
options.c: The amount of free disk space remaining on a database server.
options.d: The name of the vendor providing the firewall hardware for the network.

question: What components make up the 'ELK Stack' often used as a basic SIEM pipeline?

options.a: Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana.
options.b: Endpoint, Logs, and Kernel.
options.c: Encryption, Logging, and Keys.
options.d: External, Local, and Known.

question: Which tool provides the most granular level of control over network traffic into and out of a Linux machine?

options.a: iptables / firewalld.
options.b: Get-Service.
options.c: Services.msc.
options.d: The system log viewer.

question: What is an advantage of centralized logging?

options.a: It allows logs to be stored securely and analyzed in real-time from a single location.
options.b: It completely removes the need for local endpoint firewall rules.
options.c: It automatically patches all vulnerabilities found within the server environment.
options.d: It enables users to reset their own passwords without administrative intervention.

question: What are the common event categories that should be audited on critical systems?

options.a: Privilege changes, sensitive file access, system configuration changes, and execution of privileged commands.
options.b: Browser history, user-downloaded image files, and printer queue status.
options.c: CPU usage, memory availability, and disk read/write speeds.
options.d: The brand of the computer and the model of the local monitor display.

question: Which command is used to change file permissions on a Linux system?

options.a: chmod
options.b: chown
options.c: chgrp
options.d: chperm

question: What does a '7' represent in a Linux octal permission mode?

options.a: Read, write, and execute (rwx).
options.b: Read and execute only (r-x).
options.c: Write only (-w-).
options.d: No permissions (---).

question: What is the primary function of the 'Group Policy' feature in an Active Directory environment?

options.a: To push security configurations and rules to multiple machines from a central location.
options.b: To automatically scan for and install hardware drivers on new devices.
options.c: To create a backup of all local system data on a regular schedule.
options.d: To act as an external firewall for all outgoing internet traffic.

question: Why should you test new policies in a lab environment before deploying them?

options.a: To avoid accidental service disruption or being locked out of systems due to misconfiguration.
options.b: To ensure they are compatible with all legacy, unsupported hardware devices.
options.c: To measure the impact on network bandwidth during policy deployment.
options.d: To wait for a vendor's approval on the policy configuration.

question: What is the function of the 'Local Security Policy' tool?

options.a: To manage security settings on a standalone Windows machine that is not domain-joined.
options.b: To manage security settings across an entire corporate domain automatically.
options.c: To configure the hardware BIOS settings for an individual server.
options.d: To scan for and remove viruses from the system local drive.

question: What is the primary benefit of BitLocker's integration with the TPM (Trusted Platform Module)?

options.a: It ensures secure storage of encryption keys and validates system integrity during boot.
options.b: It allows the system to boot faster than if it were not encrypted.
options.c: It automatically clears the system memory on every restart.
options.d: It allows the encryption to be managed entirely over a wireless connection.

question: Which Windows feature classifies network connections into profiles to determine firewall rules?

options.a: Network Profiles (Domain, Private, Public).
options.b: The Local Security Group Manager.
options.c: The Windows Task Scheduler.
options.d: The BitLocker Recovery Tool.

question: What are the common classifications for updates managed in WSUS?

options.a: Critical Updates, Security Updates, and Feature Packs.
options.b: Desktop Themes, Wallpapers, and Icons.
options.c: Language Packs, Audio Drivers, and Video codecs.
options.d: User accounts, Group Policies, and Domain settings.

question: Why is it important to synchronize specific Microsoft products with WSUS?

options.a: To ensure only necessary patches for deployed software are downloaded, saving bandwidth and space.
options.b: To allow end-users to choose which patches they want to install.
options.c: To prevent Microsoft from automatically updating the WSUS server itself.
options.d: To maximize the number of updates downloaded for all possible Microsoft products.

question: Which tool provides performance monitoring and alerting for host resources?

options.a: Nagios.
options.b: Git.
options.c: The Windows Registry Editor.
options.d: The Bash shell manual page.

question: What is the main advantage of continuous monitoring for security?

options.a: It identifies anomalies and security breaches in real-time.
options.b: It completely replaces the need for a firewall.
options.c: It removes the need for administrators to perform daily check-ins.
options.d: It lowers the cost of physical server hardware.

question: Which tool is known for its web-based interface and automated network device discovery?

options.a: Zabbix.
options.b: The Linux system log viewer.
options.c: The Windows Event Log file.
options.d: The Bash echo command.

question: Why do log files need to be secured?

options.a: To protect sensitive information and prevent attackers from tampering with forensic trails.
options.b: To allow the server to process files more quickly.
options.c: To make them easier for regular users to read.
options.d: To prevent the OS from automatically deleting them.

question: What is the main role of a SIEM system?

options.a: To collect, correlate, and analyze security events from diverse sources across the enterprise.
options.b: To encrypt disk partitions using physical hardware keys.
options.c: To act as the primary database storage for all business applications.
options.d: To force system reboots when updates are detected.

question: How does correlation work in a SIEM?

options.a: By linking related events from different sources to detect sophisticated attack patterns.
options.b: By storing logs in a single location for manual human inspection.
options.c: By automatically patching software vulnerabilities as they are detected.
options.d: By providing graphical icons for all server assets.

question: What is 'Normalization' in a SIEM pipeline?

options.a: Transforming diverse log formats into a common, structured format for easier analysis.
options.b: Deleting unnecessary log fields to save space.
options.c: Renaming servers to match a standard naming convention.
options.d: Changing the timezone of the logs to match the server time.

question: Why are automated alerts valuable in security operations?

options.a: They provide timely notification of potential issues, allowing for faster response times.
options.b: They reduce the amount of data stored on the server.
options.c: They make the system look more advanced to auditors.
options.d: They replace the need for human analysts entirely.

question: What is the function of the 'Kibana' component in the ELK stack?

options.a: Providing an interface to visualize and query data stored in Elasticsearch.
options.b: Collecting logs from network devices.
options.c: Storing the index files for all system events.
options.d: Managing user permissions for database access.

question: Why must security be a continuous commitment rather than a one-time project?

options.a: Threats evolve, and vulnerabilities are continuously discovered in existing environments.
options.b: Hardening is only effective for the first week after deployment.
options.c: Most organizations don't have enough IT staff to complete security in one project.
options.d: Security software automatically expires after 30 days.

question: What does a 'Security Baseline' represent?

options.a: The expected, secure state of a system that acts as a reference for detecting unauthorized changes.
options.b: The total amount of memory available to the operating system.
options.c: The minimum number of users allowed to access a server simultaneously.
options.d: The brand of firewall used at the network perimeter.

question: Why should administrative scripts be signed?

options.a: To ensure that only trusted and unaltered scripts execute on the host.
options.b: To save disk space by compressing the script contents.
options.c: To allow the script to bypass all network firewall rules.
options.d: To force the system to perform a reboot after execution.

question: How can logs assist in the aftermath of a security incident?

options.a: They provide a forensic trail to reconstruct events and identify the root cause.
options.b: They prevent the attacker from coming back the next day.
options.c: They automatically restore deleted files to the disk.
options.d: They provide a record of all business financial transactions.

question: What is the purpose of 'Whitelisting' in application security?

options.a: To permit only explicitly trusted applications to run.
options.b: To block specific, known malicious files.
options.c: To automatically grant admin rights to all users.
options.d: To encrypt the system drive entirely.

question: Which of the following describes the principle of 'Defense in Depth'?

options.a: Implementing multiple redundant layers of security controls.
options.b: Using a single very strong firewall at the edge.
options.c: Focusing security efforts only on the server room.
options.d: Limiting security tools to only the operating system.

question: In Linux, what command would you use to change file ownership to user 'apache'?

options.a: chown apache filename
options.b: chmod apache filename
options.c: chgrp apache filename
options.d: chperm apache filename

question: Why is SSL/TLS essential for web server communication?

options.a: It encrypts data in transit to prevent interception and tampering.
options.b: It allows the server to run without a firewall.
options.c: It forces the client to download a specific browser.
options.d: It manages user session cookies automatically.

question: What is the primary risk of SQL injection in a database?

options.a: Attackers can manipulate queries to retrieve, modify, or delete sensitive data.
options.b: It causes the server to reboot unexpectedly.
options.c: It slows down the network speed significantly.
options.d: It deletes the database logs permanently.

question: What does a 'Reverse Proxy' (like NGINX) often do for application security?

options.a: It sits in front of web servers to provide security and load balancing.
options.b: It replaces the OS kernel to provide better protection.
options.c: It encrypts all local hard drive partitions automatically.
options.d: It automatically disables all internet access for the server.

question: Which Linux tool allows you to track and log system audit events?

options.a: auditd
options.b: syslog-ng
options.c: journalctl
options.d: all of the above

question: Why should administrative panels (like Tomcat Manager) be restricted?

options.a: Because they provide control over the application environment and are high-value targets.
options.b: Because they are rarely used by real administrators.
options.c: Because they consume too much CPU power when running.
options.d: Because they are incompatible with web browsers.

question: What is the goal of auditing in Windows security?

options.a: To track critical activities for forensic and compliance purposes.
options.b: To restrict user access to the internet.
options.c: To automatically patch the operating system.
options.d: To increase the system's overall storage capacity.

question: How can you implement least privilege in database access?

options.a: Granting only the necessary permissions required for each user's job.
options.b: Granting full administrator rights to all users by default.
options.c: Bypassing authentication for all internal network traffic.
options.d: Storing database passwords in plaintext configuration files.

question: What does 'brute-force' refer to in the context of system security?

options.a: Repeatedly trying different username and password combinations to guess credentials.
options.b: Physically attacking the server room door to gain entry.
options.c: Using a powerful magnets to delete data from storage drives.
options.d: Disabling the firewall by forcibly cutting network cables.

question: What is the primary role of the 'Logstash' component in the ELK stack?

options.a: Collecting and normalizing log data from multiple sources.
options.b: Visualizing logs in a dashboard.
options.c: Storing the primary log database index.
options.d: Encrypting the server drive.

question: What is a 'False Positive' in the context of security alerts?

options.a: When a security tool flags legitimate activity as suspicious or malicious.
options.b: When a malicious attack goes completely undetected.
options.c: When an attacker manages to change the server's configuration.
options.d: When a system crash is caused by an antivirus update.

question: What role does documentation play in forensic investigations?

options.a: It ensures transparency, accuracy, and support for legal or audit proceedings.
options.b: It is used only to satisfy HR requirements.
options.c: It is needed to help the server run faster.
options.d: It allows the software vendor to track license usage.

question: Which of the following is a best practice for managing system credentials?

options.a: Avoid hardcoding secrets in plain text and use secure credential storage solutions.
options.b: Use the same password for all database and application services.
options.c: Share root passwords between all members of the IT team.
options.d: Post passwords in visible locations for easier troubleshooting.

question: What is the primary function of a web server in a system environment?

options.a: To process client web requests and serve content over HTTP/HTTPS.
options.b: To manage the core operating system hardware drivers.
options.c: To act as a physical bridge for network traffic.
options.d: To store encrypted binary backups of the entire hard drive.

question: How can you audit user access in Windows?

options.a: By reviewing the Security Log in the Event Viewer.
options.b: By checking the device manager settings.
options.c: By physically inspecting the computer screen.
options.d: By reading the BIOS manufacturer manual.

question: What is the impact of failing to secure system logs?

options.a: Attackers may tamper with logs to cover their tracks during an incident.
options.b: The server will perform better without log overhead.
options.c: The OS will automatically reboot the system.
options.d: The application will consume more memory.

question: What is the benefit of using automated deployment tools like Ansible/Puppet?

options.a: They ensure consistent, repeatable configurations and reduce human error.
options.b: They completely eliminate the need for any manual server maintenance.
options.c: They are designed to monitor network traffic speed only.
options.d: They provide automatic hardware repair for broken server components.

question: Which of the following is a common security weakness in middleware configurations?

options.a: Default administrative ports and passwords left unchanged.
options.b: Overly secure encryption of all data by default.
options.c: Excessive logging that is stored on a separate secure server.
options.d: The use of advanced hardware-based biometric authentication.

question: Why should you restrict the use of administrative accounts for daily tasks?

options.a: To limit the impact if an account is compromised during routine work.
options.b: Because administrative accounts consume more CPU power.
options.c: To force users to use different password management software.
options.d: Because administrative accounts cannot be used on the internet.

question: What is the role of a 'Jail' in Fail2ban configuration?

options.a: To specify the rules for banning an IP address for a specific service and trigger event.
options.b: To hide a sensitive file from the Linux file system.
options.c: To prevent unauthorized users from shutting down the machine.
options.d: To encrypt the server's SSH key store.

question: What is the purpose of 'Whitelisting' in application security?

options.a: To permit only explicitly trusted applications to run.
options.b: To block specific, known malicious files.
options.c: To automatically grant admin rights to all users.
options.d: To encrypt the system drive entirely.

question: What does a '5' represent in a Linux octal permission mode?

options.a: Read and execute (r-x).
options.b: Read, write, and execute (rwx).
options.c: Write only (-w-).
options.d: Read only (r--).

question: What is the function of the 'Elasticsearch' component in the ELK stack?

options.a: To index and securely store the normalized log data.
options.b: To visualize the log data in a dashboard.
options.c: To collect raw logs from various server sources.
options.d: To update the software version on the servers.

question: Which of the following is a best practice for system security logs?

options.a: Send logs to a central server to ensure their integrity and aid in centralized analysis.
options.b: Store all logs on the same partition as the operating system.
options.c: Delete logs monthly to prevent the server from running out of disk space.
options.d: Keep logs only in temporary memory to prevent long-term storage requirements.

question: What makes an application server like Apache Tomcat a common target?

options.a: It hosts backend logic and is often the bridge between users and databases.
options.b: It is unable to be patched by security administrators.
options.c: It is mandatory for all Linux distributions to run it.
options.d: It provides no authentication mechanisms at all.

question: What is the benefit of a security baseline?

options.a: It provides a clear reference for what a 'secure' state looks like to identify deviations.
options.b: It limits the server to running only one application.
options.c: It automatically generates a high-quality user manual for the application.
options.d: It disables the user's ability to change system settings.

question: Why is it important to use strong cipher suites in SSL/TLS configuration?

options.a: To provide robust protection against modern cryptographic attacks.
options.b: To ensure the server works with very old browsers.
options.c: To make the application launch faster.
options.d: To reduce the CPU load on the server.

question: Which Windows tool records user activity and system events?

options.a: Event Viewer.
options.b: Device Manager.
options.c: Disk Cleanup.
options.d: System Properties.
